cases.rules_text:
1. Taking of another person's property is present when the person takes property and that property belongs to another.
2. Unlawful intent means an intent to keep the property without lawful entitlement.
3. Force or threat during the taking includes either pushing the victim during the taking or threatening immediate harm during the taking.
4. Transfer of another person's property is present when the victim transfers property and that property belongs to another person.
5. A deception-induced transfer is present when deception causes a transfer of another person's property.
6. High-value property means that the value of the property is large.
7. Owner-authorized taking is present when the person takes the property with the owner's permission.
8. Emergency property taking is present when the person takes property as necessary to avoid an immediate danger.
9. Theft conduct consists of taking another person's property together with unlawful intent.
10. Fraud conduct consists of a deception-induced transfer of another person's property together with unlawful intent.
11. The consent defense is present when the taking was authorized by the owner.
12. The necessity defense is present when the taking was necessary to avoid an immediate danger.
13. The theft article covers theft conduct; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of not more than 3 years.
14. The fraud article covers fraud conduct; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of not more than 3 years.
15. The robbery article applies when theft conduct and force or threat during the taking are both present; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 3 and 10 years.
16. The aggravated theft article applies when theft conduct and high value property are both present; the prescribed consequence is imprisonment of between 3 and 10 years.
17. The consent defense article covers consent defense; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
18. The necessity defense article covers necessity defense; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
19. Where the robbery article and the theft article both apply, the robbery article prevails.
20. Where the aggravated theft article and the theft article both apply, the aggravated theft article prevails.
21. Where the robbery article and the aggravated theft article both apply, the robbery article prevails.
22. Where the fraud article and the theft article both apply, the fraud article prevails.
23. The consent defense article operates as an exception to the theft article when both apply.
24. The consent defense article operates as an exception to the fraud article when both apply.
25. The consent defense article operates as an exception to the robbery article when both apply.
26. The consent defense article operates as an exception to the aggravated theft article when both apply.
27. The necessity defense article operates as an exception to the theft article when both apply.
28. The necessity defense article operates as an exception to the fraud article when both apply.
29. The necessity defense article operates as an exception to the robbery article when both apply.
30. The necessity defense article operates as an exception to the aggravated theft article when both apply.

8. Emergency property taking is present when the person takes property as necessary to avoid an immediate danger.
9. Theft conduct consists of taking another person's property together with unlawful intent.

11. The consent defense is present when the taking was authorized by the owner.
12. The necessity defense is present when the taking was necessary to avoid an immediate danger.
